openmeter/billing/models/creditsapplied.purpose: Pure domain model package defining CreditsApplied (named slice type) and CreditApplied (value type) representing credits applied to billing line items. Enforces positive-amount invariant and provides currency-precision-aware summation. No persistence code lives here.

patterns.name: models.Validator compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every type in this package must implement models.Validator via a Validate() error method and be verified with a compile-time assertion var _ models.Validator = (*T)(nil).

patterns.name: models.Clonable deep-copy via lo.Map
patterns.description: CreditsApplied implements models.Clonable[CreditsApplied] using lo.Map to produce a safe shallow copy of the slice. Any new slice or pointer field must be deep-copied in Clone.
patterns.example: func (c CreditsApplied) Clone() CreditsApplied { return lo.Map(c, func(item CreditApplied, _ int) CreditApplied { return item }) }

patterns.name: Currency-aware summation with RoundToPrecision
patterns.description: SumAmount rounds each item via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before accumulating. New aggregation helpers must use the calculator, not raw Add.

patterns.name: Positive-amount enforcement in Validate
patterns.description: CreditApplied.Validate() rejects non-positive amounts. Never bypass Validate before accumulating credits into billing totals.
patterns.example: if !c.Amount.IsPositive() { return errors.New("amount must be positive") }

key_file_guides.role: Single source of all types in this package. Defines CreditsApplied, CreditApplied, and all methods including Clone, Validate, SumAmount, and CloneWithAmount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditApplied.Amount must remain positive (validated). Adding nullable fields requires updating Clone and Validate. Do not add DB/Ent imports here.

anti_patterns: Storing negative credit amounts — Validate() enforces positivity; bypassing it causes billing arithmetic errors.
Accumulating amounts without RoundToPrecision — causes currency precision drift across line items.
Adding DB/Ent concerns to this package — it is a pure domain model with no persistence code.
Using == instead of .Equal() when comparing alpacadecimal.Decimal fields in any new helper.

decisions.decision: CreditsApplied is a named slice type rather than a struct with a slice field.
decisions.rationale: Allows direct iteration, len checks, and method attachment without wrapping, matching the pattern used by other billing model collections in this domain.

openmeter/billing/models/externalid.purpose: Defines external-ID domain structs (InvoiceExternalIDs, LineExternalIDs) and generic Ent mixin/helper functions for reading, writing, and mapping external app IDs (invoicing, payment) on invoice and line Ent entities. Bridges the domain model and the Ent schema without coupling either side directly.

patterns.name: Generic Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces per entity type
patterns.description: Each Ent CRUD operation has a typed generic interface (LineExternalIDCreator[T], LineExternalIDUpdater[T], LineExternalIDGetter). Adapters must call the package helpers (CreateLineExternalID, UpdateLineExternalID, MapLineExternalIDFromDB) rather than calling Set* methods directly.
patterns.example: func CreateLineExternalID[T LineExternalIDCreator[T]](creator LineExternalIDCreator[T], ids LineExternalIDs) T { return creator.SetNillableInvoicingAppExternalID(lo.EmptyableToPtr(ids.Invoicing)) }

patterns.name: lo.EmptyableToPtr for optional string fields
patterns.description: Empty strings are converted to nil pointers using lo.EmptyableToPtr when writing to Ent nullable columns. New external-ID fields must follow this pattern to avoid storing empty strings in nullable DB columns.

patterns.name: Ent mixin for schema field declarations
patterns.description: LineMixin and InvoiceMixin embed mixin.Schema and declare optional/nillable string fields. New external-ID fields must be added to the correct mixin, then corresponding getter/setter must be added to the typed interfaces.

patterns.name: Lockstep field addition across four locations
patterns.description: Adding a new external-ID field requires updating: (1) the Mixin Fields(), (2) the Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, (3) the Create/Update/Map helpers, and (4) the model.go struct (including Equal if present).
patterns.example: // See mixin.go InvoiceMixin + InvoiceExternalIDCreator[T] + CreateInvoiceExternalID + InvoiceExternalIDs struct

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin schema definitions and all generic helper functions for CRUD operations on external IDs for both invoice and line entities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new external-ID field requires updating all four locations: Mixin Fields(), Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, Create/Update/Map helpers, and model.go struct.

key_file_guides.role: Pure domain structs InvoiceExternalIDs and LineExternalIDs with nil-safe accessor methods and equality check (LineExternalIDs.Equal).
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineExternalIDs.Equal must be updated when new fields are added. GetInvoicingOrEmpty uses nil-receiver guard — replicate this pattern for new pointer-receiver accessors.

anti_patterns: Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns — always use lo.EmptyableToPtr.
Calling Set* Ent methods directly in adapters instead of using the typed helpers — breaks consistency when fields are added.
Adding business logic or validation to this package — it is purely a model/mixin utility layer.
Forgetting to update LineExternalIDs.Equal when adding a new string field to LineExternalIDs.

decisions.decision: Generic interfaces (Creator[T], Updater[T], Getter) rather than concrete Ent types.
decisions.rationale: Both invoice and line Ent entities share the same external-ID fields; generics allow a single helper to work across both entity types without duplication.

openmeter/billing/models/stddetailedline.purpose: Defines the Base struct for standard detailed billing line items (flat-fee and usage-based), the Ent schema mixin providing all DB columns, generic Creator/DBGetter interfaces for Ent mutations and mapping, and goderive-generated equality. This is the shared schema/model layer consumed by both Ent adapters and domain services — it bridges the Ent schema definition and the domain model in a single package.

patterns.name: Composite Ent mixin via entutils.RecursiveMixin
patterns.description: Mixin embeds entutils.RecursiveMixin[mixinBase] which composes entutils.AnnotationsMixin, entutils.ResourceMixin, and totals.Mixin. New fields must be added to mixinBase.Fields(), not directly to entity schemas.

patterns.name: Generic Creator[T] interface for Ent create mutations
patterns.description: Creator[T] composes externalid.LineExternalIDCreator[T], billingtotals.Setter[T], and all primitive Set* methods. The Create[T] function atomically populates all fields. New fields require updating both Creator[T] and the Create function in create.go.
patterns.example: func Create[T Creator[T]](creator Creator[T], line Base) T

patterns.name: DBGetter interface for DB-to-domain mapping
patterns.description: FromDB[T DBGetter] reads all fields from a generic DB entity through the DBGetter interface. Adding a field to Base requires adding the getter to DBGetter and updating FromDB. All time.Time fields must be normalized to UTC with .In(time.UTC).
patterns.example: func FromDB[T DBGetter](dbEntity T, taxConfig *productcatalog.TaxConfig) Base

patterns.name: goderive equality generation — never hand-edit derived.gen.go
patterns.description: Base.Equal delegates to deriveEqualBase generated by goderive. After adding fields to Base, run `go generate` in this package to regenerate derived.gen.go. The generator uses .Equal() for alpacadecimal.Decimal fields automatically.
patterns.example: func (l Base) Equal(other Base) bool { return deriveEqualBase(&l, &other) }

patterns.name: Deep clone for pointer and slice fields
patterns.description: Base.Clone explicitly deep-copies TaxConfig pointer and CreditsApplied slice. Any new pointer or slice field added to Base must be deep-copied in Clone to prevent aliasing bugs.

patterns.name: Enum Values()/Validate() pattern with compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Category implements models.Validator and uses slices.Contains against Category("").Values(). New enum types must follow the same Values()/Validate() pattern with var _ models.Validator = (*T)(nil).

key_file_guides.role: Canonical Base struct, Category enum, Comparable interface, Compare function, and BackfillTaxConfig helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields to Base requires updating: Clone, Equal (via goderive regen), DBGetter, Creator[T], FromDB, and mixin.go Fields(). All six locations must change together.

key_file_guides.role: Ent schema mixin providing all DB fields for stddetailedline entities, SQL CHECK constraints (child_unique_reference_id NOT EMPTY), and indexes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a NOT EMPTY SQL CHECK — never make it optional. credits_applied is jsonb-optional with pointer type in DB getter.

key_file_guides.role: Generic Create[T] function for populating Ent create mutations from a Base value.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with all Base fields; missing a field here silently omits it from DB writes. All time.Time values use .In(time.UTC).

key_file_guides.role: DBGetter interface and FromDB generic mapping function from DB rows to Base.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time.UTC normalization must be applied to all time fields via .In(time.UTC). GetCreditsApplied returns a pointer — dereference with lo.FromPtr.

key_file_guides.role: Goderive-generated equality helpers. DO NOT EDIT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If Base gains fields with custom equality semantics (e.g. decimal), verify generated code uses .Equal() not == after regeneration.
Run after any change to Base struct to keep derived.gen.go in sync.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing derived.gen.go — always regenerate via go generate after Base changes.
Adding DB/Ent logic to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go — keep layers strictly separate.
Omitting time.UTC normalization in FromDB for time.Time fields — causes timezone-dependent test failures.
Forgetting to update Creator[T] and DBGetter when adding a new field to Base — silently omits the field from writes/reads.
Making child_unique_reference_id optional — it has a DB CHECK constraint and is required for line upsert identity.

decisions.decision: Base struct co-located with the Ent mixin rather than in a separate domain package.
decisions.rationale: Both the Ent schema definition and the domain model must stay in lock-step; co-locating them makes drift immediately visible and allows the Creator/DBGetter interfaces to share package-level types without cross-package indirection.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal methods.
decisions.rationale: Base contains alpacadecimal.Decimal fields that require .Equal() not ==; goderive generates correct field-by-field equality automatically and stays in sync with struct changes via go generate.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new string field to Base and wire it through all layers

openmeter/billing/models/totals.purpose: Defines the Totals struct representing a billing line's financial breakdown (amount, taxes, charges, discounts, credits, total) with currency-precision rounding, additive aggregation (Add/Sum), subtraction (Sub), negation (Neg), and an Ent mixin for persisting all 8 fields as PostgreSQL numeric columns. Used by all billing line types and invoice aggregations.

patterns.name: Ent mixin with typed Setter[T]/TotalsGetter interfaces
patterns.description: totals.Mixin declares all 8 numeric DB fields. Setter[T] and TotalsGetter provide typed interfaces for Ent create/update and DB reads. Use Set[T](mut, totals) and FromDB(e) for all Ent interactions — never call individual Set*/Get* methods directly in adapters.
patterns.example: func Set[T Setter[T]](mut Setter[T], totals Totals) T { return mut.SetAmount(totals.Amount).SetTaxesTotal(totals.TaxesTotal). /* ... all 8 fields */ }

patterns.name: Currency-precision rounding via RoundToPrecision
patterns.description: RoundToPrecision applies calculator.RoundToPrecision to every field atomically, returning a new Totals value. Always call before persisting or comparing totals across currencies.

patterns.name: Additive aggregation via Add/Sum
patterns.description: Totals.Add accumulates multiple Totals values field-by-field. Use Sum(...) to aggregate a variadic list. Do not accumulate individual fields outside these helpers to avoid missing a field.

patterns.name: Non-negative invariant enforcement in Validate
patterns.description: All 8 fields must be non-negative. Validate() is called in the billing domain before persistence. New fields added to Totals must have a corresponding non-negative check in Validate.
patterns.example: if t.Amount.IsNegative() { return errors.New("amount is negative") }

patterns.name: CalculateTotal formula is fixed
patterns.description: CalculateTotal returns Amount + ChargesTotal + TaxesExclusiveTotal - DiscountsTotal - CreditsTotal. Never recompute this inline; always use CalculateTotal when deriving the final line total.
patterns.example: func (t Totals) CalculateTotal() alpacadecimal.Decimal { return alpacadecimal.Sum(t.Amount, t.ChargesTotal, t.TaxesExclusiveTotal, t.DiscountsTotal.Neg(), t.CreditsTotal.Neg()) }

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Validate, Add, Sub, Neg, Sum, Equal, IsZero, RoundToPrecision, and CalculateTotal methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new field requires updating Add, Sub, Neg, RoundToPrecision, Equal, IsZero, Validate, and CalculateTotal (if it participates in the total formula). All 8 existing methods must cover the new field.

key_file_guides.role: Ent schema mixin (Mixin struct) + Setter[T]/TotalsGetter interfaces + Set[T] and FromDB helpers for Ent CRUD.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All fields use dialect.Postgres: 'numeric'. Adding a field requires updating Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB in lockstep — all five locations.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for RoundToPrecision with USD half-up rounding semantics and Equal correctness.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use exact string comparison on rounded decimals — update expected values if precision logic changes. Add analogous test cases for new fields.

anti_patterns: Calling individual Set*/Get* Ent methods for totals fields in adapters — always use Set[T] and FromDB helpers.
Accumulating totals outside Add/Sum — individual field accumulation leads to fields being missed.
Skipping RoundToPrecision before persistence — causes numeric precision drift in multi-currency invoices.
Adding a field to Totals without updating Add, Sub, Neg, Equal, IsZero, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB.

decisions.decision: All totals fields use alpacadecimal.Decimal stored as PostgreSQL numeric.
decisions.rationale: Billing requires exact decimal arithmetic; float64 SQL types would introduce rounding errors at the storage layer.

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in the same package.
decisions.rationale: Ensures the Setter/Getter interfaces stay in sync with the Totals struct fields without cross-package indirection — a field addition immediately surfaces missing interface methods.
